features.summary: Costco reported strong Q3 fiscal 2021 results with net income of $1.22 billion ($2.75 per share) versus $838 million ($1.89 per share) in the prior year. Net sales increased 21.7% to $44.38 billion, with comparable sales up 20.6% (15.1% excluding gas and FX). Traffic increased 12.5% worldwide while average transaction size grew 7.3%. The company opened 6 new warehouses in Q3 and plans 7 more in Q4, targeting 21 net new locations for fiscal 2021.
features.management_outlook: Management expects to open approximately 25 new warehouses annually in each of the next two fiscal years, including expansion in China with a second location opening in late calendar 2021 and a third in late 2022. The company is implementing a phased return of food sampling and expanded food court operations over the coming weeks. Full-year CapEx is estimated at $3.3-3.5 billion, increased from prior guidance due to a $340 million distribution facility acquisition to support big and bulky delivery operations.
features.risks: Costco faces significant inflationary pressures across multiple categories, with overall price inflation estimated at 2.5-3.5% versus 1-1.5% three months ago. Supply chain challenges include port delays, container shortages (35-50% increase in incoming containers), and chip shortages affecting various products. Container turnaround times have doubled from 25 to 50 days, and transportation costs have increased substantially. These pressures may impact gross margins despite the company's efforts to mitigate cost increases.
features.key_drivers: Strong performance was driven by continued elevated shopping frequency and basket sizes, with executive membership growing by 817,000 members in the quarter. E-commerce sales grew 38.2% (excluding FX), with big and bulky categories up 53% supported by the Costco Logistics acquisition. The company benefited from reduced COVID-related costs ($57 million versus $283 million prior year) and strong operational leverage from high sales volumes. Membership renewal rates remained healthy at 91.0% in the US/Canada and 88.4% worldwide.
